title: Spinal cord stimulation in 60 cases of intractable pain
journal_name: Journal of Neurology, Neurosurgery, and Psychiatry
study_question: What is the efficacy and safety of spinal cord stimulation for intractable pain?
population_assessed: 60 patients with intractable pain lasting up to 50 years, ages 21-74 years.
outcome_measures: Pain relief categorized as significant benefit, modest benefit, no effect, or made worse.
inclusion_criteria: Patients with intractable pain unresponsive to all other measures tried, implanted with spinal cord stimulators.
answer_to_study_question: 47% significant benefit, 23% modest benefit, 20% no effect, 6.7% made worse. 70% derived benefit overall.
